017package org.apache.juneau.marshall.msgpack;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.CollectionUtils.*;
021import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.IoUtils.*;
022import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Shorts.*;
023
024import java.io.*;
025import java.time.*;
026import java.time.temporal.*;
027import java.util.*;
028import java.util.function.*;
029
030import org.apache.juneau.commons.bean.*;
031import org.apache.juneau.marshall.*;
032import org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.*;
033import org.apache.juneau.marshall.stream.*;
034
035/**
036 * Session object that lives for the duration of a single use of {@link MsgPackSerializer}.
037 *
038 * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
039 *    <li class='warn'>This class is not thread safe and is typically discarded after one use.
040 * </ul>
041 *
042 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
043 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/MessagePackSupport">MessagePack Basics</a>
044
045 * </ul>
046 */
047@SuppressWarnings({
048   "resource",   // Output streams managed by calling code
049   "java:S110",  // Inheritance depth acceptable for serializer session hierarchy
050   "java:S115"   // Constants use UPPER_snakeCase convention (e.g., CONST_value)
051})
052public class MsgPackSerializerSession extends OutputStreamSerializerSession implements TokenWritable, ArrayRecordWritable {
053
054   // Argument name constants for assertArgNotNull
055   private static final String ARG_ctx = "ctx";
056
057   /**
058    * Builder class.
059    */
060   public static class Builder extends OutputStreamSerializerSession.Builder<Builder> {
061
062      /**
063       * Constructor
064       *
065       * @param ctx The context creating this session.
066       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
067       */
068      protected Builder(MsgPackSerializer ctx) {
069         super(assertArgNotNull(ARG_ctx, ctx));
070      }
071
072      @Override
073      public MsgPackSerializerSession build() {
074         return new MsgPackSerializerSession(this);
075      }
076
077   }
078
079   private static class SimpleMapEntry {
080      final Object key;
081      final Object value;
082
083      SimpleMapEntry(Object key, Object value) {
084         this.key = key;
085         this.value = value;
086      }
087   }
088
089   /**
090    * Creates a new builder for this object.
091    *
092    * @param ctx The context creating this session.
093    *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
094    * @return A new builder.
095    */
096   public static Builder create(MsgPackSerializer ctx) {
097      return new Builder(assertArgNotNull(ARG_ctx, ctx));
098   }
099
100   /*
101    * Converts the specified output target object to an {@link MsgPackOutputStream}.
102    */
103   private static MsgPackOutputStream getMsgPackOutputStream(SerializerPipe out) throws IOException {
104      Object output = out.getRawOutput();
105      if (output instanceof MsgPackOutputStream output2)
106         return output2;
107      var os = new MsgPackOutputStream(out.getOutputStream());
108      out.setOutputStream(os);
109      return os;
110   }
111
112   /**
113    * Constructor.
114    *
115    * @param builder The builder for this object.
116    */
117   protected MsgPackSerializerSession(Builder builder) {
118      super(builder);
119   }
120
121   /**
122    * Opens a low-level push generator that emits MessagePack one structural event at a time,
123    * bound to this live session.
124    *
125    * <p>
126    * MsgPack containers are length-prefixed with no indefinite-length encoding, so the writer
127    * <b>buffers</b> each open container's body bytes until the matching {@code endXxx} call;
128    * memory cost is O(largest open container).
129    *
130    * <h5 class='section'>Builder properties honored:</h5>
131    * <c>keepNullProperties</c>, <c>trimEmptyMaps</c>, <c>trimEmptyCollections</c>, <c>sortMaps</c>,
132    * <c>sortCollections</c>, <c>trimStrings</c> &mdash; all flow through to
133    * {@link TokenWriter#object(Object) object(Object)}.
134    *
135    * <h5 class='section'>Builder properties NOT honored:</h5>
136    * <c>useWhitespace</c>, <c>maxIndent</c>, <c>quoteChar</c>, <c>escapeSolidus</c> (binary
137    * format); <c>uriContext</c>, <c>uriResolution</c>, <c>uriRelativity</c>, <c>listener</c>.
138    *
139    * @param output The output.
140    * @return A new {@link MsgPackTokenWriter}.
141    * @throws IOException If the output type is not supported or could not be opened.
142    */
143   @Override /* TokenWritable */
144   public TokenWriter serializeTokens(Object output) throws IOException {
145      var walk = new PojoWalker.Options(
146         isKeepNullProperties(),
147         isTrimEmptyMaps(),
148         isTrimEmptyCollections(),
149         isSortMaps(),
150         isSortCollections(),
151         isTrimStrings(),
152         getMarshallingContext());
153      return MsgPackTokenWriter.forOutput(output, new MsgPackTokenWriter.Settings(walk));
154   }
155
156   /**
157    * Buffered array-element {@link RecordWriter}.  MsgPack's array wire format is
158    * length-prefixed, so streaming without a known element count requires either buffering
159    * (this method) or the {@link #serializeArrayRecords(Object, int)} overload that takes a count.
160    *
161    * @param output The output.
162    * @return A buffered {@link RecordWriter}.
163    * @throws IOException If a problem occurred opening the underlying output.
164    */
165   @Override /* ArrayRecordWritable */
166   public RecordWriter serializeArrayRecords(Object output) throws IOException {
167      return RecordAdapter.arrayWriter(this, output);
168   }
169
170   @Override /* ArrayRecordWritable */
171   public boolean isArrayRecordStreaming() { return false; }
172
173   /**
174    * Streaming array-element {@link RecordWriter} that pre-declares the element count and
175    * writes each element directly to the output stream &mdash; no per-element buffering.
176    * The caller must pass exactly {@code expectedCount} {@link RecordWriter#write(Object)}
177    * calls before {@link RecordWriter#close()}; mismatch throws on close.
178    *
179    * @param output The output (must be an {@link OutputStream}).
180    * @param expectedCount The number of elements that will be written.
181    * @return A streaming {@link RecordWriter}.
182    * @throws IOException If a problem occurred opening the underlying output.
183    */
184   @Override /* ArrayRecordWritable */
185   public RecordWriter serializeArrayRecords(Object output, int expectedCount) throws IOException {
186      if (!(output instanceof OutputStream))
187         throw new IOException("MsgPack streaming arrayRecordWriter requires an OutputStream");
188      var os = (OutputStream) output;
189      var mpos = new MsgPackOutputStream(os);
190      mpos.startArray(expectedCount);
191      return new RecordWriter() {
192         private int written;
193         private boolean closed;
194
195         @Override public RecordWriter write(Object value) throws IOException {
196            if (closed)
197               throw new IllegalStateException("Array stream is closed.");
198            if (written >= expectedCount)
199               throw new IllegalStateException(
200                  "Array stream is full; declared " + expectedCount + " elements, attempted to write more.");
201            try {
202               MsgPackSerializerSession.this.serialize(value, os);
203            } catch (SerializeException e) {
204               throw new IOException(e);
205            }
206            written++;
207            return this;
208         }
209         @Override public boolean isStreaming() {
210            return true;
211         }
212         @Override public void flush() throws IOException {
213            os.flush();
214         }
215         @Override public void close() throws IOException {
216            if (closed)
217               return;
218            closed = true;
219            if (written != expectedCount)
220               throw new IOException(
221                  "Array stream count mismatch: declared " + expectedCount + " but wrote " + written);
222            os.close();
223         }
224      };
225   }
226
227   /*
228    * Workhorse method.
229    * Determines the type of object, and then calls the appropriate type-specific serialization method.
230    */
231   @SuppressWarnings({
232      "rawtypes",   // Raw types necessary for generic type handling
233      "java:S3776"  // Cognitive complexity acceptable for serialization dispatch logic
234   })
235   private MsgPackOutputStream serializeAnything(MsgPackOutputStream out, Object o, ClassMeta<?> eType, String attrName, BeanPropertyMeta pMeta) throws SerializeException {
236
237      if (o == null)
238         return out.appendNull();
239
240      if (eType == null)
241         eType = object();
242
243      ClassMeta<?> aType = null;       // The actual type
244      ClassMeta<?> sType = null;       // The serialized type
245
246      aType = push2(attrName, o, eType);
247
248      // Handle recursion
249      if (aType == null)
250         return out.appendNull();
251
252      // Handle Optional<X>
253      if (isOptional(aType)) {
254         o = getOptionalValue(o);
255         eType = getOptionalType(eType);
256         aType = getClassMetaForObject(o, object());
257      }
258
259      sType = aType;
260      String typeName = getBeanTypeName(this, eType, aType, pMeta);
261
262      // Swap if necessary
263      var swap = aType.getSwap(this);
264      if (nn(swap)) {
265         o = swap(swap, o);
266         sType = swap.getSwapClassMeta(this);
267
268         // If the getSwapClass() method returns Object, we need to figure out
269         // the actual type now.
270         if (sType.isObject())
271            sType = getClassMetaForObject(o);
272      }
273
274      // '\0' characters are considered null.
275      if (o == null || (sType.isChar() && ((Character)o).charValue() == 0))
276         out.appendNull();
277      else if (sType.isBean())
278         serializeBeanMap(out, toBeanMap(o), typeName);
279      else if (sType.isMap()) {
280         if (o instanceof BeanMap o2)
281            serializeBeanMap(out, o2, typeName);
282         else
283            serializeMap(out, (Map)o, eType);
284      } else if (sType.isCollection()) {
285         serializeCollection(out, (Collection)o, eType);
286      } else if (sType.isByteArray()) {
287         out.appendBinary((byte[])o);
288      } else if (sType.isArray()) {
289         serializeCollection(out, toList(sType.inner(), o), eType);
290      } else if (sType.isBoolean()) {
291         out.appendBoolean((Boolean)o);
292      } else if (sType.isNumber()) {
293         out.appendNumber((Number)o);
294      } else if (sType.isUri() || (nn(pMeta) && pMeta.isUri())) {
295         out.appendString(resolveUri(o.toString()));
296      } else if (sType.isDate()) {
297         out.appendString(serializeDate((Date)o, sType));
298      } else if (sType.isCalendar()) {
299         out.appendString(serializeCalendar(o, sType));
300      } else if (sType.isTemporal()) {
301         out.appendString(serializeTemporal((TemporalAccessor)o, sType));
302      } else if (sType.isDuration()) {
303         appendDuration(out, (Duration)o);
304      } else if (sType.isPeriod()) {
305         out.appendString(serializePeriod((Period)o));
306      } else if (sType.isStreamable()) {
307         // MsgPack protocol requires array size in header (startArray(size)), so materialization is unavoidable.
308         serializeCollection(out, toListFromStreamable(o, sType), eType);
309      } else if (sType.isReader()) {
310         pipe((Reader)o, out, SerializerSession::handleThrown);
311      } else if (sType.isInputStream()) {
312         pipe((InputStream)o, out, SerializerSession::handleThrown);
313      } else
314         out.appendString(toString(o));
315
316      pop();
317      return out;
318   }
319
320   private void serializeBeanMap(MsgPackOutputStream out, BeanMap<?> m, String typeName) throws SerializeException {
321
322      Predicate<Object> checkNull = x -> isKeepNullProperties() || nn(x);
323
324      var values = new ArrayList<BeanPropertyValue>();
325
326      if (nn(typeName)) {
327         BeanPropertyMeta pm = m.getMeta().getTypeProperty();
328         values.add(new BeanPropertyValue(pm, pm.getName(), typeName, null));
329      }
330
331      m.forEachValue(checkNull, (pMeta, key, value, thrown) -> {
332         if (nn(thrown)) {
333            onBeanGetterException(pMeta, thrown);
334            return;
335         }
336         var p = new BeanPropertyValue(pMeta, key, value, null);
337
338         if ((! isKeepNullProperties()) && willRecurse(p)) {
339            return; // Must handle the case where recursion occurs and property is not serialized.
340         }
341
342         values.add(p);
343      });
344
345      out.startMap(values.size());
346
347      values.forEach(x -> {
348         BeanPropertyMeta pMeta = x.getMeta();
349         if (pMeta.canRead()) {
350            ClassMeta<?> cMeta = (ClassMeta<?>) x.getBeanInfo();
351            String key = x.getName();
352            Object value = x.getValue();
353            serializeAnything(out, key, null, null, null);
354            serializeAnything(out, value, cMeta, key, pMeta);
355         }
356      });
357   }
358
359   @SuppressWarnings({
360      "rawtypes",  // Raw types necessary for generic collection handling
361      "unchecked"  // Type erasure requires unchecked operations
362   })
363   private void serializeCollection(MsgPackOutputStream out, Collection c, ClassMeta<?> type) throws SerializeException {
364      var elementType = type.getElementType();
365      List<Object> l = listOfSize(c.size());
366      c = sort(c);
367      l.addAll(c);
368      out.startArray(l.size());
369      l.forEach(x -> serializeAnything(out, x, elementType, "<iterator>", null));
370   }
371
372   @SuppressWarnings({
373      "rawtypes",  // Raw types necessary for generic map handling
374      "unchecked"  // Type erasure requires unchecked operations
375   })
376   private void serializeMap(MsgPackOutputStream out, Map m, ClassMeta<?> type) throws SerializeException {
377
378      var keyType = type.getKeyType();
379      var valueType = type.getValueType();
380
381      m = sort(m);
382
383      // The map size may change as we're iterating over it, so
384      // grab a snapshot of the entries in a separate list.
385      List<SimpleMapEntry> entries = listOfSize(m.size());
386      m.forEach((k, v) -> entries.add(new SimpleMapEntry(k, v)));
387
388      out.startMap(entries.size());
389
390      entries.forEach(x -> {
391         Object value = x.value;
392         Object key = generalize(x.key, keyType);
393         serializeAnything(out, key, keyType, null, null);
394         serializeAnything(out, value, valueType, null, null);
395      });
396   }
397
398   private boolean willRecurse(BeanPropertyValue v) throws SerializeException {
399      var aType = push2(v.getName(), v.getValue(), (ClassMeta<?>) v.getBeanInfo());
400      if (nn(aType))
401         pop();
402      return aType == null;
403   }
404
405   @Override /* Overridden from SerializerSession */
406   protected void doSerialize(SerializerPipe out, Object o) throws IOException, SerializeException {
407      serializeAnything(getMsgPackOutputStream(out), o, getExpectedRootType(o), "root", null);
408   }
409
410   @Override
411   public boolean isAddBeanTypes() { return super.isAddBeanTypes(); }
412
413   private void appendDuration(MsgPackOutputStream out, Duration value) {
414      var f = getDurationFormat();
415      var s = serializeDuration(value);
416      if (f == DurationFormat.NANOS || f == DurationFormat.MILLIS)
417         out.appendNumber(Long.parseLong(s));
418      else if (f == DurationFormat.SECONDS)
419         out.appendNumber(Double.parseDouble(s));
420      else
421         out.appendString(s);
422   }
423}
